plans.name: Admin 00 - Operating Contract
plans.overview: Lock the admin product direction, scope boundaries, source-of-truth order, and cut rules before widening workflow parity work.

todos.content: Confirm the direction: custom apps/admin replaces the default Medusa dashboard over time without forking Medusa Admin.
Confirm frontend-first scope: use existing Medusa Admin API by default and document backend gaps before touching medusa-be.
Confirm source order: current app, official Medusa docs, local Medusa dashboard clone, project backend custom routes, repo-local skills.
Lock cut order: broad redesign and rare settings screens are cut before orders/products/customers operational workflows.

plans.name: Admin 01 - Boundary ADR and Guardrails
plans.overview: Keep the admin scope safe by recording backend, UI kit, Medusa parity, and verification rules in an ADR and app-scoped agent guidance.

todos.content: Maintain the admin boundary ADR covering apps/admin scope, backend-gap rules, UI kit usage, and Medusa parity meaning.
Maintain apps/admin/AGENTS.md with guardrails for sources, UI kit rules, planning, verification, and backend escalation.
Document when to load Medusa, source-driven, UI kit, plan-graph, dag, simplify, and testing skills.
List prohibited patterns: CORS workarounds, fake data, unmanaged local primitives, backend edits without a gap, and broad redesign drift.

plans.name: Admin 02 - Source Map and Shared Contracts
plans.overview: Build the source map and shared API/type/rule contracts that later parity lanes depend on.

todos.content: Map default Medusa dashboard routes/components for settings, orders, products, customers, and extensions from the local Medusa clone.
Map Admin API endpoints, fields, filters, mutations, and auth expectations needed by the first parity lanes.
Map existing project custom admin endpoints in medusa-be for emails, Packeta, PPL, Payload, QR payments, producers, and order operations.
Stabilize shared admin types, query keys, response normalization, and business rules before domain lanes widen.

plans.name: Admin 03 - Shell and Settings Parity
plans.overview: Reproduce the default dashboard shell and settings workflows that make the custom admin feel like a complete admin surface.

todos.content: Implement store/account menu behavior including store settings entry and logout parity.
Implement settings navigation groups: General, Developer, My Account, and Extensions.

Add read views for store details, store currencies/locales, metadata/JSON, users, regions, tax regions, reasons, sales channels, product types/tags, locations, translations, API keys, workflows, profile, and extension settings as supported by current API.
Add only the safest high-value settings mutations after source mapping confirms request contracts, starting with store edit and then staged currencies/locales/metadata updates.

plans.name: Admin 04 - Orders Workflow Parity
plans.overview: Turn the current order list/detail into a working operational surface for common Medusa and project-specific order workflows.

todos.content: Map default Medusa order list/detail/actions and project custom order endpoints before adding mutations.
Complete order detail sections for customer, addresses, items, totals, payment, fulfillment, metadata, notes, and linked records.
Add safe confirmed actions first: order email send, address/email edits, shipment views, order export, filters/search, project operation links, and non-destructive activity/metadata views.
Add cancel, capture, fulfill, shipment creation, refund, return, exchange, claim, order edit, transfer, or confirm/receive only after backend contract and deployed-data safety are explicit.

plans.name: Admin 05 - Products Workflow Parity
plans.overview: Turn the product list/detail into a useful product management surface with carefully staged edit capabilities.

todos.content: Map default Medusa product list/detail/create/edit routes, forms, fields, and mutation endpoints.
Complete product detail read sections for variants, options, images, categories, sales channels, shipping profile, stock, metadata, and organization.
Add basic edit flows for title, subtitle, handle, status, description, organization, and metadata after contracts are confirmed.
Add variants, pricing, media, and inventory edits only after source mapping and validation rules are explicit.

plans.name: Admin 06 - Customers and B2B Workflow
plans.overview: Complete customer visibility and B2B approval workflows around the original badge requirement.

todos.content: Map default Medusa customer list/detail routes and project-specific B2B customer metadata/status conventions.
Add customer detail read view for identity, contact, groups/company data, addresses, orders, metadata, and B2B status.
Keep the B2B pending filter and sidebar badge aligned with the RAW acceptance criteria.
Add approve/reject actions only after existing backend semantics or required backend gap are documented.

plans.name: Admin 07 - Extensions Workflow Parity
plans.overview: Bring project-specific extension screens to operational parity: emails, Packeta, PPL, Payload, QR payments, Meilisearch, producers, content, image gallery, and order operations.

todos.content: Map existing project custom admin endpoints and current screens for each extension.
Harden existing email logs, Packeta labels, PPL, Payload, Packeta config, and QR payment screens.
Plan and implement missing high-value extension screens: Meilisearch, producers, content, image gallery, and order operations.
Document mutation side effects and safe smoke-test boundaries for each extension.

plans.name: Admin 08 - Quality, Deploy, and Verification
plans.overview: Integrate finished lanes, verify against deployed backend, and keep the admin safe to review on ZaneOps.

todos.content: Run targeted Biome, typecheck, and build for apps/admin changes.
Maintain a smoke matrix covering login, badges, orders, products, catalog taxonomy, customers, settings, and extension screens.
Verify key workflows against the deployed test backend and record any backend config gaps.
Write PR/deploy notes with scope, tested routes, risky actions not executed, and next lanes.

plans.name: Admin 09 - Catalog Taxonomy Workflow Parity
plans.overview: Add default-admin parity for catalog taxonomy routes that sit beside products: categories, collections, category ranking, category product assignment, metadata, and project category description widgets.

todos.content: Map default Medusa category and collection route trees, data hooks, forms, and project widgets against the deployed default admin.
Implement category list and detail read parity including search, active/public status, products table, organize sidebar, metadata, and JSON.
Implement category create, edit, ranking/organize, product assignment, and metadata edit only after request contracts are confirmed.
Port the project category description editor mounted at product_category.details.before, preserving top/bottom description metadata keys and rich HTML editor behavior.
Implement collection list/detail/create/edit/product assignment/metadata parity as a parallel catalog slice after category read parity is stable.

Add variants, pricing, media, and inventory edits only after source mapping and validation rules are explicit.
